title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Fresh trust-and-safety signal is thin today, with most provided items unrelated to AI governance or safeguards. The clearest relevant updates are new research on privacy leakage from conversational AI logs, how LLM explanations can unduly shape human decisions, and grounding failures in vision-language models used for embodied tasks.

items.title: Inferential Privacy Leakage in Anonymized Conversational AI Logs
items.why_it_matters: It suggests anonymized chat histories may still reveal sensitive traits, raising deployment and data-governance risks.

items.title: Human Decision-Making with Persuasive and Narrative LLM Explanations
items.why_it_matters: It examines whether fluent AI explanations can steer users beyond warranted trust in model outputs.

items.title: SPACENUM: Revisiting Spatial Numerical Understanding in VLMs
items.why_it_matters: It probes whether VLM numeric outputs are truly grounded, relevant for reliability in embodied deployments.

items.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
items.why_it_matters: It addresses differential-privacy budget overuse and value attribution in multi-agent data-sharing systems.

items.title: On the Stability of Spherical Hellinger-Kantorovich Flows and Their Implications for Differential Privacy
items.why_it_matters: It could inform the theoretical robustness of privacy-preserving sampling methods used in sensitive ML settings.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
